data.narrative:
NVision Imaging Technologies is a Series A biotech company that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 companies across Biotech & Health—3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers at Series B stage, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression uses the $20M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying $40M. Peer-Median anchors to the median valuation across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers, which stands at $805M; this peer set ranges from $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp anchoring cannot be applied because NVision's revenue is not disclosed. The public comparables trade at a median 5.8x price-to-sales, but lacks the data necessary to apply this multiple.

The triangulated range spans $34M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $422M across the two applicable methods. The substantial width reflects stage-progression's conservatism relative to peer medians. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the Series B peer cohort for a Series A valuation and whether NVision's technology differentiation warrants a premium or discount to peer medians.
